title: Review the post-change documentation risk in /Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public: which user/operator-facing docs now contradict the new default assist=none path? Prioritize anything that could mislead operations or make tests/checks fail. Do not edit files; return concise findings with fi...
summary_text: **Findings (docs contradicting new `assist=none` default path)** 1. High: Quickstart/config in [README.md:137](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/README.md:137) and [README.md:200](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/README.md:200) still frame assist as default `claude` (`FUGUE_ASSIST_ORCHESTRATOR_PROVIDER --body claude`, “assist既定は claude”). 2. High: Resolution contract in [AGENTS.md:78](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/AGENTS.md:78) still declares assist fallback default `claude`. 3. High: Operator flow doc [docs/gha24-mainframe-flow.md:10](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/gha24-mainframe-flow.md:10) explicitly says “Assist defaults to `claude` (co-orchestrator on by default).” 4. Medium: Requirements contract in [docs/requirements-codex-main-claude-assist.md:39](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/requirements-codex-main-claude-assist.md:39) still specifies hard fallback assist `claude`; [docs/requirements-codex-main-claude-assist.md:114](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/requirements-codex-main-claude-assist.md:114) still recommends `claude` as policy default. **Why this is risky (ops/check impact)** 5. High check/regression risk: these docs conflict with executable/default contract in [scripts/lib/orchestrator-policy.sh:24](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/orchestrator-policy.sh:24) and regression expectation [tests/test-orchestrator-policy.sh:181](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/tests/test-orchestrator-policy.sh:181) (`empty defaults to codex+none`), so operators/devs following docs can misconfigure runs or “fix” code/tests in the wrong direction.
